prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
The Quarter of the Third Sun hath closed, and the ledger-scrolls speak of a great bounty, though no grain was harvested nor castle raised; instead, they count a multitude of "seats" in a spectral "cloud," a most curious and immaterial treasury.

Many lords of distant lands did renew their oaths of fealty to our platform, yet I have surveyed the grounds and found no platform, only humming silver boxes wherein captive imps are said to perform the calculations.

The Grand Reeve of Coin proclaims a "net retention" of one hundred and ten pieces of a hundred, a sorcery of numbers that suggests we somehow gathered more coin from the same vassals without increasing their tribute, a paradox that vexes my quill.

Behold, our greatest trial: the "churn," a dread wasting sickness not of men, but of custom, where subscribers vanish from the rolls as silently as spirits, leaving behind naught but an empty seat in the aforementioned, and utterly invisible, cloud.
